err: title too short (14 chars, need 15) - e.g. 'Fix login timeout' not 'Fix bug'
flag accessed but not defined: desc

flags.description: Scaffold the Astro + USWDS project and establish the initial page structure for Ride Conditions.
Build the core ride journal form, validation, review, and save flow using USWDS patterns.
Resolve addresses to coordinates and use National Weather Service data to display ride conditions.
Add AWS Amplify data persistence for saved ride entries, with auth/owner access if feasible for MVP.
Write usage guidance, architecture notes, and accessibility evidence for the portfolio presentation.

flags.acceptance: Astro app runs locally. USWDS CSS loads globally. A sample USWDS button or form control is visible. Setup notes are added to README.
flags.description: Create the base Astro application, install USWDS, wire global styles/scripts, and confirm one USWDS component renders.

flags.acceptance: Routes exist for home, journal, rides, and docs. Navigation identifies current page. Skip link and main landmark are present.
flags.description: Create home, journal, saved rides, and documentation routes with accessible navigation.

flags.acceptance: Every field has an associated label. Grouped controls use fieldsets and legends. Required fields are visible and programmatic. Form works by keyboard.
flags.description: Implement the ride journal form using USWDS form patterns for date, address, distance, duration, effort, weather notes, and ride notes.

flags.acceptance: Invalid fields show actionable errors. Errors are associated with their fields. Focus moves to meaningful error content after failed submission.
flags.description: Add client-side validation for required fields and numeric values using USWDS-compatible error states.

flags.acceptance: Review page or panel shows all entered ride details. Edit returns to form with values preserved. Focus management is sensible after continue/edit/save actions.
flags.description: Add a review step so users can confirm normalized ride details before saving.

flags.acceptance: Provider decision is recorded in ARCHITECTURE.md. Environment variables are identified if needed. Manual coordinate fallback is documented.
flags.description: Select the address lookup strategy for MVP and document limits, attribution, setup, and fallback behavior.

flags.acceptance: Address lookup returns latitude, longitude, and display label. Loading and error states are visible. Failed lookup does not lose form input.
flags.description: Convert entered addresses into normalized display labels and coordinates for the ride journal flow.

flags.acceptance: Points endpoint is called with coordinates. Forecast URL is extracted and fetched. Weather service handles loading, unavailable, and error outcomes.
flags.description: Use coordinates to call the NWS points endpoint, follow the forecast URL, and normalize the nearest useful forecast period.

flags.acceptance: Panel shows temperature, wind, short forecast, and a derived condition label. Empty and error states use USWDS alerts. Source attribution is documented or visible.
flags.description: Display weather summary in a USWDS-styled panel near the journal flow.

flags.acceptance: Amplify files exist. Local setup steps are in README. Sandbox deployment path is documented. Secrets or generated outputs are handled intentionally.
flags.description: Add the Amplify backend foundation for data persistence and document the local sandbox workflow.

flags.acceptance: Model includes ride fields, coordinates, notes, weather snapshot fields, and timestamps. Required fields are explicit. Auth or owner-access choice is documented.
flags.description: Define the persisted ride entry schema for journal entries and weather snapshots.

flags.acceptance: Confirmed ride saves through Amplify. Save loading state is visible. Save failure shows an accessible alert. Form data is not lost on failure.
flags.description: Persist confirmed ride journal entries through Amplify and handle save states accessibly.

flags.acceptance: Saved rides page reads entries from Amplify. Each entry shows date, address, distance, effort, notes preview, and weather snapshot. Empty and loading states are present.
flags.description: Display persisted ride entries in a scan-friendly saved rides page.

flags.acceptance: Docs include when to use, when not to use, content guidance, validation guidance, accessibility notes, and implementation notes or code snippet.
flags.description: Create a documentation page explaining when and how to use the ride journal pattern.

flags.acceptance: ARCHITECTURE.md reflects final geocoder choice, Amplify responsibilities, NWS data flow, and known MVP tradeoffs.
flags.description: Keep architecture notes current as the implementation decisions are made.

flags.acceptance: Keyboard results are recorded. VoiceOver findings are recorded. axe findings are recorded. Lighthouse accessibility score is recorded. Follow-up fixes are filed in td.
flags.description: Complete automated and manual accessibility checks and record results for the portfolio.

flags.reason: independent review passed: build, local run, USWDS wiring, sample component, README notes
